title: New proofs for technical results in "Infinitesimal invariants of mixed Hodge structures'' (arXiv:2406.17118v1)
abstract: Cubic forms $C$ are constructed in the work of R. Aguilar, M. Green and P. Griffiths to establish the generic global Torelli theorem for Fano-K3 pairs $(X,Y)$, where $X: F=0$ is a cubic threefold in $\mathbb{P}^4$ and $Y\in|-K_X|$ is an anticanonical smooth section of $X$ defined by a quadratic form $Q$. In this article, we prove the following two results, which were previously verified with the computer aid of Macaulay2: for a generic pair $(X,Y)$, (i) the cubic form $C$ is smooth; (2) $(J_{F,3}:Q)=0$, and thereby give a precise meaning of the word ``generic" in this context.
